steps.title: Use the motive clue
steps.content: Meier says the climbers' claims were self-interested. Since edelweiss already symbolized strength and courage, claiming the flowers grew only in dangerous places would make the climbers who reached them seem impressive.

steps.title: Match the climbers' benefit
steps.content: B is unlikely because the story made edelweiss seem difficult to reach. C treats the claims as scientific observations, but the passage calls them self-interested. D concerns abundance, not danger. The climbers would benefit by appearing brave and strong. The answer is A.
